headline: Foreign spouse visa delays average 14 months for processing
context: Foreign spouses of Malaysian citizens waited an average of 14 months for long-term visit pass processing in 2024, affecting family unity.

cards.big: Foreign spouses wait average 14 months for long-term visit pass in Malaysia
cards.sub: 5,400 applications pending as of December 2024 at Immigration Department

cards.big: Immigration Act 1959 grants director general discretion on passes without timelines
cards.sub: No statutory right to family reunification or maximum processing period

cards.big: ICCPR Article 23 protects family as fundamental group unit of society
cards.sub: Malaysia has not ratified ICCPR but family rights recognised in Constitution

cards.big: 63% of delayed applications involved spouses from Bangladesh, Indonesia, and Philippines
cards.sub: Processing times vary significantly by nationality of foreign spouse

cards.big: When visa processing takes 14 months, families are punished for cross-border love
Immigration Department launched online tracking system in March 2025
